summary.narrative: Governor Dunleavy campaigned on five core priorities: repealing Senate Bill 91 (criminal justice reform), fighting for a full Permanent Fund Dividend (PFD), reducing state spending, developing natural resources (particularly Alaska LNG), and improving education outcomes. He achieved significant crime reduction (31% overall, 15.9% violent crime 2019–2023) and successfully repealed SB91. He signed executive orders on government efficiency and regulatory reform. Progress on Alaska LNG accelerated in 2025, and he signed wage exemption repeal legislation. Implementation varies across priorities; criminal justice and regulatory reform show concrete results, while fiscal sustainability and education outcomes remain contested. Bipartisan coalitions in legislature have constrained some executive initiatives.
summary.grade_rationale: Strong delivery on criminal justice reform (SB91 repeal, crime reduction) and regulatory efficiency; meaningful progress on energy development. Limited evidence of sustained improvement in education outcomes or achievement of structural fiscal plan. PFD policy delivery unclear from sources. Bipartisan legislature constraints and competing priorities reduced implementation scope relative to campaign ambitions.

promises.normalized: Reduce crime rates through public safety initiatives
Develop Alaska LNG project for energy exports
Implement government efficiency review and reform
Grow Alaska economy and open state for business
Improve school outcomes and education results

data_gaps: Specific details on PFD policy delivery and amount changes achieved
Education outcome metrics and improvement targets (test scores, graduation rates)
Fiscal sustainability plan details and implementation timeline
State spending reduction targets and actual budget cuts realized
Alaska LNG project timeline, funding status, and partnership agreements
Natural resource development licensing and permitting metrics
Legislative effectiveness data and bills passed by session
Public approval ratings and favorability trends
Economic growth metrics (GDP, job creation, unemployment rate)
Detailed state trooper hiring numbers and public safety staffing
